content.problemStatement:
Chef and his friend Bharat have decided to play the game "The Chefora Spell".

In the game, a positive integer NNN (in decimal system) is considered a "Chefora" if the number of digits ddd is **odd** and it satisfies the equation N=‚ąĎi=0d‚ąí1Ni‚čÖ10i,N = \sum_{i=0}^{d-1} N_i \cdot 10^i,N=‚ąĎi=0d‚ąí1‚ÄčNi‚Äč‚čÖ10i,

where NiN_iNi‚Äč is the iii-th digit of NNN from the left in 000-based indexing.

Let AiA_iAi‚Äč denote the iii-th **smallest** Chefora number.

They'll ask each other QQQ questions, where each question contains two integers LLL and RRR. The opponent then has to answer with

(‚ąŹi=L+1R(AL)Ai)‚Äämod‚Ää109+7.\left(\prod_{i=L+1}^R (A_{L})^{A_{i}}\right) \bmod{10^9+7}.(‚ąŹi=L+1R‚Äč(AL‚Äč)Ai‚Äč)mod109+7.

Bharat has answered all the questions right, and now it is Chef's turn. But since Chef fears that he could get some questions wrong, you have come to his rescue!


Input Format

The first line contains an integer QQQ - the number of questions Bharat asks.
Each of the next QQQ lines contains two integers LLL and RRR.


Output Format
Print QQQ integers - the answers to the questions on separate lines.
